title: Racosperma seclusum
intro: Racosperma seclusum, also known as Acacia seclusa, is a species of shrub or small tree in the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine, and its health benefits include the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma seclusum include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Racosperma seclusum is often made into a tea or infused as a decoction, and it is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its effects, although its use is not as widespread as some other Acacia species.

This family is one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom, comprising over 19,000 species.
